---
title: "LRP70: Do verbal/reading trajectories grow together, and does baseline non-verbal ability predict the common tempo? (shared growth-tempo factor)"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRP70 is the **shared growth-tempo** companion to LRP69 (issue #187, Q5). It keeps
LRP69's joint growth-curve structure and adds a rank-1 **shared child-level
growth-tempo factor** $G_i \sim \mathrm{Normal}(0,1)$ loading (positively, for
identification) on every measure's slope. This is the genuinely *joint* layer — it
tests two questions the independent-core LRP69 cannot:

1. **Do the measures grow together?** The loadings $\lambda_m$ say how strongly each
   measure's growth rate tracks a common developmental tempo.
2. **Does baseline non-verbal ability predict the common tempo?** Read out post-hoc
   as the correlation between each child's latent tempo and their block-design score.

`LOO(LRP69 vs LRP70)` shows whether the shared tempo earns its keep at n≈54. If
LRP70 will not sample cleanly, LRP69 is the primary deliverable and the factor is
reported as not estimable at this sample size (as the RI-CLPM companion was for
LRP67).

_Symbol mapping (read before the tables)._

| Construct | Symbol | Measure | Denominator |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Receptive vocabulary | **R** | `rowpvt` | 170 |
| Expressive vocabulary | **E** | `eowpvt` | 170 |
| Receptive grammar | **T** | `trog` | 32 |
| Word reading | **W** | `ewrswr` | 79 |
| Letter-sound knowledge | **L** | `yarclet` | 32 |

Non-verbal ability is **block design** (`blocks`), recorded at t1 only, complete
for all 54 children.

## Model structure

As LRP69, with the slope gaining a shared growth-tempo term $\lambda_m G_i$:

$$
\text{slope}_m[i] = \beta_m + \gamma_m\,z(\text{blocks}_i)
                   + \lambda_m\,G_i + \sigma^1_m z^1_m[i],
\qquad G_i \sim \mathrm{Normal}(0,1),\;\; \lambda_m \ge 0.
$$

$\gamma_m$ is now the block-design → growth-rate association **incremental to** the
common tempo; $\lambda_m$ is the tempo loading; the intercept, the masked
Beta-Binomial likelihood and the omitted dose covariate are unchanged from LRP69.
The positive-loading constraint identifies the factor's sign (higher $G$ = faster
growth on every measure); it is a parsimonious rank-1 stand-in for cross-measure
slope covariation, **not** an identified general-ability construct.

{{< include _partials/_results_growth.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
